notes.anchor: the day before the Ides of Sextilis
notes.note: Sextilis was the old Roman name for the month later renamed August (in 8 BC) in honour of Augustus; the Ides fell on the 13th, so this is 12 August 47 BC. The conciliatory letter from Caesar mentioned here was the signal that Cicero's long wait at Brundisium was nearly over.
